title: fraxinus ornus subsp. cilicica
intro: Fraxinus ornus subsp. cilicica, also known as the Turkish Ash or Anatolian Ash, is a subspecies of Fraxinus ornus, a deciduous tree native to the Anatolia region of Turkey. The plant's bark, leaves, and seeds have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus ornus subsp. cilicica are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and aiding in the management of diabetes. Herbal preparations of the plant are often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and are sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
